title: Delegation Routing
instruction: Route three project emails to the correct team leads. Read each email to figure out who should handle it based on the content. For the customer complaint, BCC avery.patel@leadership.test so they are aware without the customer seeing. Star the customer complaint as the most urgent. Create a 'Delegated' label and apply it to all three forwarded emails.

emails.subject: Unresolved billing dispute — customer escalation
emails.body:
A customer has escalated a billing dispute that has been open for two weeks. This needs support lead review — please forward to Avery Nguyen (avery.nguyen@support.test).

The customer is threatening to cancel their contract if we do not resolve this by end of week.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Q2 budget allocation question
emails.body:
We have a question about the Q2 budget allocation for the vendor program. I think the CFO should weigh in — Sofia Brooks (sofia.brooks@finance.test) would know whether the variance is within the approved range.

Can you route this to the right person?

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: API rate limiting issue on staging
emails.body:
We are hitting rate limits on the staging API. The CTO's team needs to review this — please forward to Priya Rivera (priya.rivera@engineering.test) so they can adjust the throttle configuration.

This is blocking the QA cycle for the next release.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Cross-team alignment — Q2 planning
emails.body:
Sharing the cross-team alignment doc for Q2 planning. No action needed — this is just for your awareness.

The working group already signed off on the proposal last Friday.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Priya

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Sofia

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Elena
